title: Checkpoint.find_resumable_run ignores user_id parameter
description: Checkpoint.find_resumable_run(user_id, run_date) accepts user_id but never uses it in the SQL query. The LIKE pattern '%-{run_date}-%' matches any run on that date regardless of user. If traces.db is ever shared across users, this could return runs belonging to a different user. The dead parameter is also misleading — callers expect user-scoped filtering.
requirements: find_resumable_run filters results to only pipeline_run_ids that belong to the given user_id
The pipeline_run_id format already encodes user or the query must use another column to scope by user
done_criteria: test_find_resumable_run_scopes_by_user passes: inserting runs for two users returns only the matching user's run
Existing runner.py usage of find_resumable_run is unaffected
context: pipeline_run_id format is '{pipeline_type}-{run_date}-{uuid6}' (orchestrator/pipeline.py:72). The LIKE pattern uses run_date but user_id is never embedded in run_id. Consider adding user_id column to checkpoints table or embedding it in run_id.
